model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Kentucky holds a slight edge in overall efficiency metrics and defensive rating compared to Santa Clara, which has a potent offense but a weaker defense that could be exploited by the Wildcats' balanced attack. While Santa Clara's 26-8 record and WCC championship show they can win, Kentucky's experience in the tougher SEC and superior defensive metrics provide a more reliable path to victory in this neutral-site matchup. The underdog's path relies on hot three-point shooting and forcing turnovers, but Kentucky's disciplined defense and efficiency make them the more reliable pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Team Quality and Efficiency Metrics
reasoningStep.summary: Kentucky ranks 28th in KenPom and 32nd in Torvik, while Santa Clara ranks 35th in KenPom and 28th in Torvik, showing a close overall quality but with Kentucky's defense being significantly better.
reasoningStep.evidence: Kentucky's adjD is 99.1 (KenPom) and 99.94 (Torvik), compared to Santa Clara's 104.3 and 103.66, indicating a defensive edge for the Wildcats.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Kentucky vs. Santa Clara: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Kentucky vs. Santa Clara: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for Kentucky vs. Santa Clara. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: Kentucky vs. Santa Clara Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Kentucky 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Mark Pope’s second season in Lexington has been nothing but adversity management, and it didn’t quite produce the results the fanbase expected. Kentucky started the year ninth in the AP Preseason Poll, then it was seemingly all downhill from there with the injuries that have hit this squad. Kentucky went 9-4 with the 50th-ranked offense in non-conference play. Then, SEC play started, and the Wildcats ended up as a 9-seed in the conference tournament, playing in the first round against LSU. The problem is that the roster never found its footing for an entire month-long stretch. Injuries created lineup inconsistencies that made it hard to build any real continuity, and a 2-5 finish to the regular season raised fresh questions about this group’s ceiling. This is an all-around team propped up by Otega Oweh that has competed against one of the toughest schedules in hoops. Kentucky does everything reasonable well but nothing spectacularly, ranking in the top 75 in offensive turnover rate and offensive rebound rate, and 102nd in effective field goal percentage. Over their last month of play, the Wildcats went 4-5, ranking outside the top 40 on both ends of the court. Kentucky’s brand is bigger than this team, and the Wildcats lack the talent to make a deep run. Pope will have them ready to compete. Whether that’s enough to make it to the second weekend is left to be discovered. Santa Clara 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Herb Sendek’s Broncos had their best season in decades, finishing 26-8 overall and reaching the WCC Tournament championship game

No. 7 seed Kentucky and No. 10 seed Santa Clara play Friday, March 20, in a March Madness NCAA Tournament first-round game . The Midwest Region game is scheduled for 9:15 a.m. MST and can be seen on CBS ( stream with FUBO ). Who will win the March Madness game and advance to the second round in the NCAA Tournament bracket ? Check out these March Madness picks and March Madness predictions for the men's college basketball NCAA Tournament matchup . Kentucky is a 2.5-point favorite over Santa Clara in the game in March Madness odds provided by BetMGM Sportsbook . The Wildcats are -150 on the moneyline. The Broncos are +125. The over/under for the game is set at 160.5 points. Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Kentucky vs. Santa Clara: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for Kentucky vs. Santa Clara. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: Kentucky vs. Santa Clara Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Kentucky 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Mark Pope’s second season in Lexington has been nothing but adversity management, and it didn’t quite produce the results the fanbase expected. Kentucky started the year ninth in the AP Preseason Poll, then it was seemingly all downhill from there with the injuries that have hit this squad. Kentucky went 9-4 with the 50th-ranked offense in non-conference play. Then, SEC play started, and the Wildcats ended up as a 9-seed in the conference tournament, playing in the first round against LSU. The problem is that the roster never found its footing for an entire month-long stretch. Injuries created lineup inconsistencies that made it hard to build any real continuity, and a 2-5 finish to the regular season raised fresh questions about this group’s ceiling. This is an all-around team propped up by Otega Oweh that has competed against one of the toughest schedules in hoops. Kentucky does everything reasonable well but nothing spectacularly, ranking in the top 75 in offensive turnover rate and offensive rebound rate, and 102nd in effective field goal percentage. Over their last month of play, the Wildcats went 4-5, ranking outside the top 40 on both ends of the court. Kentucky’s brand is bigger than this team, and the Wildcats lack the talent to make a deep run. Pope will have them ready to compete. Whether that’s enough to make it to the second weekend is left to be discovered. Santa Clara 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Herb Sendek’s Broncos had their best season in decades, finishing 26-8 overall and reaching the WCC Tournament championship game
